metric_value: More worried than ever about lack of control over networked information assets

metric_value: Firewalls not designed for: complex web-enabled apps, limiting intranet access, segregating parties, or blocking admin access
notes: Security-savvy IS managers cite four specific firewall limitations; precursor to zero-trust critique

metric_value: Integrated off-the-shelf security solutions are nowhere to be found
notes: IS managers forced to spend time and budget assembling component point solutions

metric_value: Single platform consolidating: app-level firewall, auth server, anti-virus, encryption, web server, mail, news

notes: Nine discrete security functions consolidated onto one AViiON server

metric_value: Three-region compartmentalization: network, admin, application/data; mandatory access controls; role-based access
notes: Exceeds RACF, ACF2, TOP SECRET mainframe controls; viruses and admins cannot access production data

metric_value: Negligible performance degradation reported by IS managers; users unaware security controls operating

notes: Critical differentiator; security typically degrades performance but B2SO transparent to users

metric_value: Existing applications can be reused on Cybershield without modification; ISV libraries identical to standard Unix

metric_value: Integrated platform strategy: one-stop-shopping for intranet/e-commerce security vs. component assembly

metric_value: IS executives deploying Intranet and electronic commerce applications requiring OS-level security

metric_value: Isolates network traffic; prevents unauthorized access from external networks

metric_value: Prevents administrators from acquiring access to production applications and data

metric_value: Protects applications and data; isolates viruses from production systems

metric_value: IS executives would be well-served to evaluate DG Cybershield for protecting enterprise Intranets

metric_value: Integrated platforms will outperform component point-solutions for enterprise internet security

metric_value: E-commerce will require OS-level security controls and role-based compartmentalization to be viable

metric_value: Industry validated: unified security platforms (Checkpoint, Cisco PIX, Palo Alto NGFW) dominated; point-solution assembly declined
notes: Aberdeen's integrated-platform thesis proved correct; zero-trust architecture extends the concept further by 2020s

metric_value: OS-level and role-based security became industry standard; RBAC, OAuth2/OIDC, and zero-trust all validate 1996 thesis
notes: NIST RBAC model formalized 2000s; zero-trust architecture (Forrester 2010, NIST SP 800-207 2020) echoes B2SO concepts
